description: Canonical parameter dictionary for voltage comparators, synthesized from a real-datasheet corpus (TI LM393/LM339/TLV3501/LMV7219/TL331, Analog Devices/Maxim MAX999/MAX9140/LT1116/LT1394, onsemi LM311, ST TS391) spanning three sub-shapes that share one measurement envelope: (1) general_purpose / low-power (LM393, LM339, LM311, TLV3011) whose headline is a modest propagation delay and micro-power supply current; (2) high_speed (TLV3501, LMV7219, MAX999, LT1394, LMH7220) whose headline is a nanosecond-scale propagation_delay, propagation_delay_dispersion, and maximum_toggle_frequency; and (3) precision (LT1097-class, LTC6752, TLV3201) whose headline is a low input_offset_voltage / drift and defined input_hysteresis. A part's sub_type selects which optional keys dominate; the input DC precision (offset, bias, CMRR, PSRR), the output stage, and the power/thermal keys are common. The output stage comes in three flavours captured by the output group: open_collector and open_drain parts populate output_low_voltage (swept over I_OUT sink current) and output_leakage_current and have no output_high_voltage (the high level is set by an external pull-up); push_pull parts populate both output_low_voltage and output_high_voltage (headroom from each rail) and short_circuit_current. Roughly 80% of the input-side vocabulary is shared verbatim with the op_amp dictionary so cross-family extraction normalizes to one key; a comparator differs by having hysteresis, a switching-time family (propagation delay, dispersion, toggle frequency), and a saturating digital output instead of a linear one. A comparator has NO closed-loop / slew / distortion / noise-density behaviour to specify - open_loop_gain is a single characterized DC figure (comparators print it as V/mV, normalize to the V/V altUnit), not a load-swept curve. Some parts integrate a voltage reference (LTC1440-class, TLV3011/3012, comparator-with-reference); its keys live in the reference group (reference_output_voltage, reference_temperature_drift, reference_line/load_regulation, reference_output_current, reference_output_noise) copied from the voltage_reference vocabulary so a downstream tool treats the on-chip datum identically to a standalone reference. component.polarity is bipolar for a dual-supply comparator; store rail-relative common-mode and output levels as the headroom magnitude from the rail with the printed form in conditionsVerbatim. Condition axes for this family: T_A, V_CM (common-mode input), V_OVERDRIVE (input overdrive voltage past the trip point - the axis the headline propagation_delay is swept over, since delay collapses as overdrive grows), C_L (output load capacitance, a co-condition on every switching-time spec), I_OUT (output sink/source current, the axis output_low_voltage is swept over), F (frequency), DIRECTION (note-only: rising / falling for an edge, source / sink for an output current) and PACKAGE (for per-package thermal resistance). Pin functions: IN_P / IN_N (non-inverting / inverting inputs), OUT (comparator output), VS_POS / VS_NEG (dual supply), GND (single-supply ground / V-), SHDN (shutdown / enable), REF (integrated-reference output), HYST (external hysteresis-programming pin), LATCH (output latch/enable strobe), NC (no connect). Base-SI throughout: a 40 ns propagation delay is 4.0e-08 s, a 2.5 mV offset is 2.5e-03 V; keep the printed symbols in aliases and the printed rail-relative / dBm / per-mA forms in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Input offset voltage
parameters.hint: Differential input offset - the trip-point error at the inputs, at T_A = 25 degC unless a temperature range is given. The headline for a precision comparator. Often specified as a +/- magnitude; store as min = -X, max = +X. Grade suffixes set this number; use one document per grade.

parameters.name: Input offset voltage drift
parameters.hint: Temperature coefficient of the input offset voltage - how much the trip point moves with temperature. Precision comparators put a low uV/degC figure here.

parameters.name: Input offset current
parameters.hint: Difference of the two input bias currents. Often array over T_A. Symmetric +/- values map to min/max.

parameters.name: Input bias current
parameters.hint: Average input bias current, temperature-dependent so one measurement per T_A point. Bipolar-input comparators (LM393) draw tens of nA; CMOS-input parts draw pA.

parameters.name: Input hysteresis voltage
parameters.hint: Built-in input hysteresis - the difference between the rising and falling trip points that prevents output chatter near the threshold. Many high-speed parts specify a few mV of internal hysteresis; parts with a HYST pin let it be set externally (note the pin/resistor in conditionsVerbatim). Distinct from input_offset_voltage.

parameters.name: Common-mode input voltage range
parameters.hint: The input common-mode range over which the comparator trips correctly. Frequently rail-relative (e.g. (V-) to (V+)-1.5, or includes a rail on a ground-sensing part); store the headroom from each rail and preserve the printed form in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Common-mode rejection ratio
parameters.hint: Rejection of a common-mode input, tested over a stated V_CM range. Usually dB; some vendors give it as a uV/V ratio (V/V).

parameters.name: Power-supply rejection ratio
parameters.hint: Rejection of a supply change referred to the input offset. dB for most parts; precision parts often spec it as an offset shift in uV/V (V/V).

parameters.name: Voltage gain (open-loop)
parameters.hint: Large-signal DC voltage gain - the gain from input to output before the output saturates, a single characterized figure (a comparator is not compensated for closed-loop use, so this is not a load-swept curve). Comparators print it as V/mV; a V/mV or V/uV figure normalizes to the dimensionless V/V altUnit.

parameters.name: Propagation delay
parameters.hint: THE headline dynamic spec of a comparator: the delay from the input crossing the trip point to the output responding. It collapses as the input overdrive grows, so emit one measurement per overdrive point (V_OVERDRIVE, e.g. 5 mV, 20 mV, 100 mV) rather than one merged range. Output load capacitance C_L is a co-condition. Low-to-high (tPLH) and high-to-low (tPHL) edges differ - separate them with a DIRECTION note (rising / falling).

parameters.name: Propagation delay dispersion
parameters.hint: The spread in propagation delay across a range of input overdrive and/or slew rate - the timing-jitter figure that matters in a data-slicer or trigger. Note the overdrive/slew range it is measured across in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Maximum toggle frequency
parameters.hint: The maximum output toggle rate the comparator sustains at a stated overdrive and load. High-speed parts spec tens to hundreds of MHz.

parameters.name: Output rise / fall time
parameters.hint: 10-90 % output edge time driving the stated load capacitance C_L. Rising and falling separable via a DIRECTION note.

parameters.name: Output low (saturation) voltage
parameters.hint: The output-low level with the output sinking current. On an open-collector / open-drain part VOL scales with the sink current, so emit one measurement per I_OUT sink point (DIRECTION = sink). Store the low-level voltage magnitude referred to GND / V-.

parameters.name: Output high voltage
parameters.hint: Push-pull outputs only: the output-high level with the output sourcing current, stored as the headroom from the upper rail (open-collector / open-drain parts have no VOH - the high level is set by an external pull-up). One measurement per I_OUT source point (DIRECTION = source); preserve the printed rail-relative form in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Output sink current
parameters.hint: The current the output can sink while holding a stated output-low voltage - the drive capability of an open-collector / open-drain (or push-pull) output.

parameters.name: Output leakage current
parameters.hint: Open-collector / open-drain parts: the leakage into the high-impedance (off) output when the pull-up holds it high. Sets the maximum pull-up value for a given logic-high level.

parameters.name: Output short-circuit current
parameters.hint: Push-pull outputs: current into an output short. Source and sink can differ; use one measurement per direction with a DIRECTION note.

parameters.name: Supply voltage
parameters.hint: The total supply voltage (V+ minus V-) range: recommended for the operating range, absolute_max for the stress limit. A dual-supply part states it as a +/- magnitude.

parameters.name: Quiescent / supply current
parameters.hint: Supply current with the output in a defined state, per comparator (note when the datasheet gives it per channel vs total). Micro-power parts draw a few uA; high-speed parts several mA. Often array over T_A.

parameters.name: Shutdown supply current
parameters.hint: Supply current when the comparator is disabled via its shutdown / enable pin.

parameters.name: Enable / shutdown threshold voltage
parameters.hint: Logic thresholds of the shutdown / enable (SHDN) pin. Use min for the guaranteed-on level and max for the guaranteed-off level, or one measurement per level.

parameters.name: Turn-on / turn-off (enable) time
parameters.hint: Enable / disable delay of the shutdown pin - the time from asserting enable to a valid output. Use a DIRECTION note for on vs off.

parameters.name: Input voltage (absolute maximum)
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum voltage at an input. Frequently rail-relative ((V-)-0.3 to (V+)+0.3); keep the printed form in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Differential input voltage (absolute maximum)
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum voltage between the two inputs. Comparators often withstand the full supply differentially (no input clamp diodes) - a key distinction from an op-amp; capture the stated limit.

parameters.name: Power dissipation
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum internal power dissipation, package-dependent.

parameters.name: Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Per-package junction-to-ambient thermal resistance.

parameters.name: Operating temperature range
parameters.hint: Specified operating temperature range (recommended), or the wider absolute-maximum operating range.

parameters.name: Junction temperature (absolute maximum)
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum junction temperature.

parameters.name: Storage temperature range
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum storage temperature range.

parameters.name: ESD, human body model
parameters.hint: ESD withstand, human body model. Store as absolute_max.

parameters.name: ESD, charged device model
parameters.hint: ESD withstand, charged device model (or field-induced CDM). Store as absolute_max.

parameters.name: Integrated reference output voltage
parameters.hint: Comparators with an integrated reference (LTC1440-class, TLV3011/3012): the on-chip reference/datum the input is compared against or that is brought out on a REF pin. Store the nominal with min/typ/max from its initial-accuracy spec at T_A = 25 degC. Copied from the voltage_reference vocabulary so a downstream tool treats it identically to a standalone reference.

parameters.name: Integrated reference temperature drift
parameters.hint: Temperature coefficient of the integrated reference output over a stated T_A range - the reference analog of ppm/degC. Give the temperature range as a T_A condition.

parameters.name: Integrated reference line regulation
parameters.hint: Fractional change of the integrated reference output per volt of supply change, ppm/V (or V/V as printed). Keep the printed rate in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Integrated reference load regulation
parameters.hint: Fractional change of the integrated reference output per amp of load on the REF pin, ppm/A (or ohm as printed). Keep the printed rate in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Integrated reference output current
parameters.hint: The current the integrated reference (REF pin) can source while staying in regulation, distinct from the comparator's own output drive.

parameters.name: Integrated reference output noise
parameters.hint: Broadband output noise of the integrated reference over a stated bandwidth, usually 0.1 Hz to 10 Hz peak-to-peak. Set the statistic field and keep the bandwidth in conditionsVerbatim (convert uVpp to volts).
